jokes.text: Sarah Fitzpatrick, uh, Michelle Fitzpatrick, Claire Fitzpatrick... the list goes on.
jokes.setup: Claims he was 'getting it on with lots of chicks' back in New Zealand and starts listing names
jokes.punchline: All three women have the same last name, suggesting they might be sisters or related
jokes.explanation: Well-structured misdirection that builds expectation of sexual prowess then deflates it with the implication he may have been dating sisters

jokes.text: I don't seem to get with any women, I just talk about getting with women. - Yeah, but the ones you talk about are hot.
jokes.setup: Jemaine admits he only talks about getting with women rather than actually doing it
jokes.punchline: Bret completely misses the point and focuses on how hot the imaginary women are
jokes.explanation: Shows Bret's obliviousness to the actual problem while maintaining the show's dry humor

jokes.text: We didn't need a map. We just live down there.
jokes.setup: They're looking for Dave's place
jokes.punchline: Reveals they live in the same building, making navigation absurdly simple
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective observation humor about unnecessary preparation

jokes.text: I don't want to be a dick but could you guys, like, move away? I'm trying to look lonely.
jokes.setup: Dave asks them to move away at his own party
jokes.punchline: He's strategically trying to appear lonely to attract women
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - manufacturing loneliness as a dating strategy, delivered with perfect awkward politeness

jokes.text: Chicks are attracted to lonely guys. It's a psychological analogy.
jokes.setup: Dave explains his lonely strategy
jokes.punchline: Uses 'psychological analogy' incorrectly, likely means 'psychology' or 'analysis'
jokes.explanation: Character-specific malapropism that shows Dave's pseudo-intellectual pretensions

jokes.text: You look good, but you look like you're waiting.
jokes.setup: Dave is trying to look attractively lonely
jokes.punchline: Bret's honest assessment deflates the strategy with brutal accuracy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bret-style literal observation that accidentally destroys Dave's entire plan

jokes.text: Have you tried the gulab jamuns? They're off the hook.
jokes.setup: Dave is at a party trying to look cool and lonely
jokes.punchline: He enthusiastically recommends Indian desserts with dated slang
jokes.explanation: Shows Dave's awkward attempt to be hip while genuinely excited about party snacks

jokes.text: After six or seven weeks girls find me boring. But I'm not sure what happens, because I mean that's about how long it takes to get to know someone.
jokes.setup: Jemaine explains his relationship pattern
jokes.punchline: He's completely oblivious to the connection between getting to know him and finding him boring
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Jemaine's complete lack of self-awareness about his own personality

jokes.text: Can you move away, please? - Sure. How long for? - 30 minutes.
jokes.setup: Dave asks them to move away so he can look lonely
jokes.punchline: Bret treats it like a practical scheduling request and Dave gives a specific timeframe
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the absurd loneliness strategy with perfectly deadpan logistics

jokes.text: Looking 'round the room, I can tell that you are the most beautiful girl in the... room
jokes.setup: Standard romantic song opening about being the most beautiful girl
jokes.punchline: Awkward pause and repetition of 'room' reveals the limited scope of the compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of romantic songs with hilariously qualified compliment that sets tone for entire number

jokes.text: In the whole wide room
jokes.setup: Following up on 'most beautiful girl in the room'
jokes.punchline: Doubles down on the limited scope with mock-grandiose language
jokes.explanation: Effective callback that reinforces the absurd specificity of the compliment

jokes.text: And when you're on the street, depending on the street, I bet you are definitely in the top three good-looking girls on the street
jokes.setup: Expanding the compliment beyond the room
jokes.punchline: Immediately qualifies it with multiple conditions and downgrades to 'top three'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that gets progressively less flattering while maintaining romantic song structure

jokes.text: Depending on the street
jokes.setup: Reinforcing the conditional nature of the compliment
jokes.punchline: Emphasizes how many variables affect this 'romantic' assessment
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that reinforces the absurd conditionality

jokes.text: What is she doing at my mate's place? How did Dave get a hottie like that to a party like his?
jokes.setup: Romantic song continues with seeing the girl at the party
jokes.punchline: Breaks romantic tone to genuinely question Dave's ability to attract attractive women
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Jemaine's surprise at Dave's social success

jokes.text: Good one, Dave. Ooh, you're a legend, Dave
jokes.setup: In middle of romantic song to a woman
jokes.punchline: Breaks to genuinely compliment Dave for his party-hosting skills
jokes.explanation: Funny interruption of romance for genuine appreciation of Dave's hosting

jokes.text: He says he doesn't mind, but I can tell he kind of minds, but I'm gonna do it anyway
jokes.setup: Asking Dave's permission to approach the girl
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges Dave's obvious discomfort but proceeds anyway
jokes.explanation: Shows Jemaine's self-awareness about social dynamics but complete disregard for them

jokes.text: You could be a waitress
jokes.setup: Building up to a big compliment about her beauty
jokes.punchline: Suggests the mundane job of waitressing as the height of her potential
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of romantic song expectations with hilariously modest career suggestion

jokes.text: You could be an air hostess in the '60s
jokes.setup: Following the waitress compliment
jokes.punchline: Slightly upgrades to air hostess but qualifies it to a specific past decade
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that's still backhanded - better job but only in the past

jokes.text: You could be a part-time model
jokes.setup: Building to what seems like a real compliment
jokes.punchline: Only part-time modeling - the most qualified praise he can muster
jokes.explanation: Iconic line that perfectly captures the show's style - qualified compliment that became culturally quotable

jokes.text: It's 12:02, just me and you, and seven other dudes around you on the dance floor
jokes.setup: Setting up romantic moment - 'just me and you'
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals they're surrounded by seven other men
jokes.explanation: Effective deflation of romantic moment with specific realistic detail

jokes.text: Let's get in a cab, I'll buy you a kebab
jokes.setup: Romantic gesture of leaving together
jokes.punchline: Offers the least romantic food possible as a romantic gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jemaine's idea of romance is wonderfully unromantic

jokes.text: I can't believe that I'm sharing a kebab with the most beautiful girl I have ever seen with a kebab
jokes.setup: Romantic declaration about sharing food
jokes.punchline: Qualifies it to only girls he's seen while eating kebabs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant continuation of the qualified compliment theme with perfect absurdist specificity

jokes.text: You're so beautiful, like a tree, or a high-class prostitute
jokes.setup: Romantic compliment comparing her to something beautiful
jokes.punchline: Compares her to a tree and a prostitute as if they're equivalent compliments
jokes.explanation: Peak absurdist humor - the juxtaposition is so unexpected and delivered so sincerely it's brilliant

jokes.text: You could be a part-time model, but you probably still have to keep your normal job
jokes.setup: Returning to the part-time model compliment
jokes.punchline: Explains the practical limitations of part-time modeling
jokes.explanation: Great callback that makes the part-time model comment even less flattering

jokes.text: A part-time model, spending part of your time modeling, and part of your time next to me
jokes.setup: Continuing the part-time model theme
jokes.punchline: Presents spending time with him as equivalent career achievement to modeling
jokes.explanation: Shows Jemaine's inflated sense of his own romantic value

jokes.text: My place is usually a little tidier than this
jokes.setup: Bringing a date home
jokes.punchline: Immediate awkward excuse-making about the state of his apartment
jokes.explanation: Relatable awkwardness but fairly standard dating humor

jokes.text: Turn the light off, Bret. - No no no. It's okay. Leave it on.
jokes.setup: Jemaine wants romantic lighting
jokes.punchline: Sally immediately contradicts him, preferring to see what's happening
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment showing the mismatch in their expectations

jokes.text: So on or off with the light then?
jokes.setup: Awkward interruption of romantic moment
jokes.punchline: Bret treats the lighting as a practical decision requiring consensus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bret moment - completely missing the romantic context and treating it as logistics

jokes.text: I walked on the outside of her. I bought her a kebab. I paid for half the taxi.
jokes.setup: Jemaine listing what he did right on the date
jokes.punchline: His idea of romantic gestures includes walking position and splitting cab fare
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Jemaine's mechanical approach to romance

jokes.text: Should I have paid for the whole taxi?
jokes.setup: Trying to figure out where he went wrong
jokes.punchline: Focuses on the most minor financial detail as potential dealbreaker
jokes.explanation: Shows Jemaine's complete misunderstanding of what went wrong

jokes.text: Yeah, I think it might also be because she and I used to go out.
jokes.setup: Jemaine analyzing why Sally left
jokes.punchline: Bret casually drops the crucial information that he's Sally's ex
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan revelation that completely recontextualizes the entire situation

jokes.text: Yeah. It's 'cause you and her used to go out, but also because of the thing with the light.
jokes.setup: Learning Bret is Sally's ex
jokes.punchline: Still insists the lighting issue was equally important
jokes.explanation: Shows Jemaine's stubborn refusal to accept the obvious explanation

jokes.text: But I think it's mainly because her and I used to go out... for, like, six months. - Yeah, well... yeah, it's mainly because you used to go out, but also mainly because of the whole situation with the light.
jokes.setup: Bret emphasizing the six-month relationship
jokes.punchline: Jemaine insists both things are 'mainly' responsible
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jemaine's illogical insistence on multiple 'main' causes

jokes.text: Yeah, and you also don't want to be startled by a light, do you?
jokes.setup: Bret pointing out seeing your ex is worse
jokes.punchline: Jemaine treats sudden light exposure as equally traumatic
jokes.explanation: Continues Jemaine's absurd prioritization of minor inconveniences

jokes.text: It doesn't work.
jokes.setup: Murray asking them to try his building gadget
jokes.punchline: Bret's blunt assessment after presumably trying it
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan moment

jokes.text: Murray, present. See? Even if I do it.
jokes.setup: Jemaine questioning the roll call logic
jokes.punchline: Murray demonstrates by calling himself present as if it proves the system works
jokes.explanation: Shows Murray's complete misunderstanding of Jemaine's logical point

jokes.text: You're both aware I wasn't invited? - No. - Jemaine, did you know? You did know? - Yeah. - You know, eh? - Yeah. - Yeah. Thanks.
jokes.setup: Murray asking about Dave's party
jokes.punchline: Discovers Jemaine knew he wasn't invited, creating awkward revelation
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe moment with Murray's hurt feelings

jokes.text: What fan base? - The fan base of the band. - You mean Mel? - That's not a fan base, that's just a woman.
jokes.setup: Murray discussing increasing their fan base
jokes.punchline: Bret points out their 'fan base' is literally one person
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of Murray's grandiose language with harsh reality

jokes.text: Yeah, but I'm calling it a fan base from now on. It's just easier when I call, because if you say, 'Oh, the fan will be there,' they can tell there's only one person.
jokes.setup: Being called out for exaggerating their following
jokes.punchline: Murray's transparent attempt to make one fan sound like more through terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Murray moment showing his delusional management tactics

jokes.text: There was a typo in the ad. It was sand they wanted. Sand, you know, that wavy font? It looks like a 'B,' but it was an 'S.'
jokes.setup: Explaining why the aquarium gig didn't work out
jokes.punchline: The aquarium wanted a sand band, not the band, due to typography confusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd explanation that's both plausible and ridiculous

jokes.text: But I sent the demo. - So the good news is that, uh... they liked it. They might play it in the lobby.
jokes.setup: Disaster of booking confusion
jokes.punchline: Murray spins it as good news that they'll play their music in the lobby
jokes.explanation: Shows Murray's desperate attempt to find silver lining in complete failure

jokes.text: Oh, hey guys. - Hey, Mel. - Hey, Mel. - Hey, whoa. Crazy meeting you here, huh? - What, outside our house?
jokes.setup: Mel acting surprised to run into them
jokes.punchline: Bret points out they're literally outside their own house
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Mel's fake surprise with logical observation

jokes.text: I went to your gig. I was at the Aquarium for hours just waiting. They wouldn't let me stay after 1:00 a.m.
jokes.setup: Mel explaining missing them Tuesday
jokes.punchline: Waited at the aquarium until 1 AM for a gig that was cancelled
jokes.explanation: Shows the real-world consequences of Murray's booking incompetence

jokes.text: Well, the fish were beautiful. They were breeding... it's mating season and I... I saw fish make love.
jokes.setup: Mel trying to find positive in her wasted evening
jokes.punchline: Gets uncomfortably detailed about fish reproduction
jokes.explanation: Typical Mel oversharing that makes everyone uncomfortable

jokes.text: You... you just came from home. - Yeah, we're just popping out for a second and then, um, that's all we were going to do... just get some air.
jokes.setup: Mel pointing out they said they were going home
jokes.punchline: Jemaine's increasingly desperate excuse-making about their simple errand
jokes.explanation: Shows their desperation to avoid Mel but with weak execution

jokes.text: It's a good cake, man. - It doesn't make any difference... Isabella. - I don't want cake... - It's top quality. - Get out. I don't want your cake! - 20 bucks. Give me the TV.
jokes.setup: Someone trying to sell cake door-to-door
jokes.punchline: Escalates to demanding the TV in exchange for cake
jokes.explanation: Random NYC absurdist moment but lacks character integration

jokes.text: What an idiot, trying to sell me a cake. - It was a beautiful cake. - Yeah, it was.
jokes.setup: Dave dismissing the cake salesman as an idiot
jokes.punchline: Jemaine and Bret genuinely appreciated the cake despite the bizarre sales pitch
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their earnest appreciation of quality

jokes.text: Hey, Jemaine, I don't know how they do things back in England... - New Zealand. - Yeah, whatever. I don't really give a shit.
jokes.setup: Dave making cultural observation about Jemaine's behavior
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals he doesn't care enough to get the country right
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Dave's casual American indifference to foreign distinctions

jokes.text: Well, there was four of them so more of a love square, but, you know, no one gets on.
jokes.setup: Murray mentioning Fleetwood Mac love triangle
jokes.punchline: Corrects himself to 'love square' with mathematical precision
jokes.explanation: Good Murray moment applying bureaucratic precision to rock history

jokes.text: Mind you, they did make some of their best music back then. - 'Rumors.' - No, that's all true.
jokes.setup: Murray mentioning Fleetwood Mac's famous album 'Rumours'
jokes.punchline: Jemaine misunderstands, thinking Murray means the stories are just rumors
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on the album title that works on multiple levels

jokes.text: I don't think that's a proper camera, Murray. - Yeah, a mobile-phone camera. - I think it's mostly a phone.
jokes.setup: Murray using his phone to shoot their music video
jokes.punchline: Bret's understated assessment of the inadequate equipment
jokes.explanation: Good understatement about their low-budget production values

jokes.text: Those are your function buttons for the robot. - They look like nipples. - Don't touch them!
jokes.setup: Murray explaining his robot costume design
jokes.punchline: Bret points out the buttons look like nipples, Murray gets defensive
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about Murray's poor costume design choices

jokes.text: It doesn't look like Daft Punk. - We wanted ones like Daft Punk. - I don't know who he is.
jokes.setup: Complaining about the robot costumes not matching their reference
jokes.punchline: Murray admits he doesn't know who Daft Punk is
jokes.explanation: Shows Murray's incompetence as a manager who doesn't research

jokes.text: The distant future, the year 2000
jokes.setup: Setting up futuristic robot scenario
jokes.punchline: The year 2000 as 'distant future' when the show aired in 2007
jokes.explanation: Clever temporal joke that gets funnier as time passes

jokes.text: We no longer say 'yes', instead we say 'affirmative' - Yes, affirmative
jokes.setup: Robots explaining their linguistic evolution
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts the rule by saying 'yes' while explaining it
jokes.explanation: Good self-contradictory robot logic joke

jokes.text: Unless it's a more colloquial situation with a few robo-friends
jokes.setup: Explaining robot language rules
jokes.punchline: Robots have casual friend groups with relaxed linguistic protocols
jokes.explanation: Humanizes robots with social dynamics and casual speech

jokes.text: We used poisonous gases and we poisoned their asses
jokes.setup: Describing robot uprising against humans
jokes.punchline: Forced rhyme using 'asses' as anatomical target for poison gas
jokes.explanation: Juvenile but effective rhyme that fits the amateur song structure

jokes.text: The humans are dead - He's right they are dead - The humans are dead - They look like they're dead - It had to be done - I'll just confirm they're dead - So that we could have fun - Affirmative, I poked one, it was dead
jokes.setup: Robots celebrating human extinction
jokes.punchline: Increasingly thorough confirmation methods ending with physical poking
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with robotic thoroughness becoming absurdly methodical

jokes.text: So you get more than one 'one'? - Some people are lucky. I've had a few ones.
jokes.setup: Bret questioning the logic of multiple soulmates
jokes.punchline: Jemaine treats 'the one' as a quantifiable category he's experienced multiple times
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on romantic clichés with Jemaine's serial relationship history

jokes.text: So how many ones can you have? - Five. - How many have you had? - Three.
jokes.setup: Continuing the quantified soulmate discussion
jokes.punchline: Jemaine has specific numerical limits and current count
jokes.explanation: Good escalation treating romance like a collectible card game

jokes.text: How's that going? - It's going pretty good. I still need to get more body in the hair. - It pretty much looks like a helmet.
jokes.setup: Asking about progress on the hair helmet
jokes.punchline: Bret honestly assessing that it fails its basic purpose
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up showing the project's obvious failure

jokes.text: It's helmet-hair night, man, not really. I mean, I've been thinking about it for ages. It's on the board.
jokes.setup: Jemaine asking him to reschedule his project
jokes.punchline: Bret treats his absurd hobby with scheduling seriousness and planning board
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Bret's systematic approach to bizarre projects

jokes.text: You know, it is actually a bit weird with you here, Bret.
jokes.setup: Jemaine insisting it won't be weird
jokes.punchline: Sally immediately proves Bret right about the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the argument that vindicates Bret's prediction

jokes.text: You do look beautiful, I was just about to say that. - Bret, this is my date.
jokes.setup: Jemaine complimenting Sally after Bret does
jokes.punchline: Sally has to remind him he's on a date with her
jokes.explanation: Shows the awkward dynamic and Sally's frustration

jokes.text: I wouldn't have ordered the duck. I spent all my money on the duck. Would you be able to bring the duck home?
jokes.setup: Bret leaving the awkward dinner
jokes.punchline: Prioritizes not wasting his expensive duck order over graceful exit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bret moment prioritizing practical concerns over social grace

jokes.text: I just... sometimes I think the thing you're looking for is often standing right in front of your eyes.
jokes.setup: Mel comforting Bret about Sally
jokes.punchline: Obviously hinting that she's interested in him
jokes.explanation: Predictable romantic cliché but fits Mel's stalker character

jokes.text: You can tell me anything. Anything. I promise I won't think you're a pervert.
jokes.setup: Mel offering emotional support
jokes.punchline: Brings up perversion unprompted, suggesting that's where her mind goes
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mel's inappropriate boundaries

jokes.text: Have I shown you my picture of Jemaine's lips? I have it here in my wallet. Look. Those are my cousins, and there... that's... that's Jemaine's lips.
jokes.setup: Mel trying to comfort Bret
jokes.punchline: Reveals she carries a photo of Jemaine's lips in her wallet
jokes.explanation: Peak Mel stalker behavior that's disturbing and hilarious

jokes.text: Whenever I was watching the two of you it was obvious that you were more into her than she was into you. Yeah, you could really tell. She always looked kind of bored.
jokes.setup: Mel trying to make Bret feel better about Sally
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment that makes him feel worse
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mel's poor social skills despite good intentions

jokes.text: You looked kind of cute when I was drunk. - Maybe what I really want is an Australian.
jokes.setup: Sally explaining her attraction was alcohol-influenced
jokes.punchline: Suggests she wants someone from a different country entirely
jokes.explanation: Adds insult to injury by wanting different nationality

jokes.text: You can't break my heart, it's liquid. It melted when I met you.
jokes.setup: Transitioning into breakup song
jokes.punchline: Presents liquid heart as defense against heartbreak
jokes.explanation: Beautiful absurdist metaphor that's both romantic and logically flawed

jokes.text: I'm not crying, it's just been raining on my face
jokes.setup: Song about not crying
jokes.punchline: Absurd excuse attributing tears to localized weather
jokes.explanation: Iconic line that perfectly captures the show's absurdist approach to emotion

jokes.text: And if I am crying, it's not because of you, it's because I'm thinking about friend of mine that you don't know who is dying
jokes.setup: Further denying the tears are about the breakup
jokes.punchline: Invents elaborate alternative explanation involving fictional dying friend
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of denial with increasingly elaborate excuse

jokes.text: I've just been cutting onions, I'm making a lasagna for one
jokes.setup: More excuses for tears
jokes.punchline: Classic onion excuse but emphasizes lonely single portion
jokes.explanation: Combines cliché excuse with pathetic detail about cooking alone

jokes.text: For your information, there's an inflammation in my tear gland
jokes.setup: Medical excuse for crying
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-scientific explanation for emotional response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with medical technobabble

jokes.text: My eyes are just a little sweaty today, they've been looking around a lot, searching for you
jokes.setup: Physical excuse for watery eyes
jokes.punchline: Accidentally admits he's been looking for her everywhere
jokes.explanation: Brilliant excuse that undermines itself by revealing emotional truth

jokes.text: What did you cook? - Are you trying to get back together with Sally? - No. Just making chitchat. - Oh, okay. Macaroni.
jokes.setup: Bret asking detailed questions about Jemaine's past with Sally
jokes.punchline: Accepts the 'chitchat' explanation and answers about macaroni
jokes.explanation: Shows both characters' social dynamics - Bret's obvious interest and Jemaine's obliviousness

jokes.setup: Announcing a guitar solo in a robot song
jokes.punchline: Solo consists of counting in binary instead of music
jokes.explanation: Brilliant concept - guitar solo replaced by robot counting, perfectly executed

jokes.text: Come on, sucker, lick my battery
jokes.setup: Robot song getting more aggressive
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo but with robot anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good robot-specific innuendo that fits the character
